title: Size effects on generation-recombination noise
verified_location: Eqs. 1-3; terminal current; analytical spectra and limits
terminal_boundary_condition: fixed-voltage current noise; conduction plus displacement current
psd_convention: source convention translated in project audit
main_assumptions: unipolar; uniform coefficients; analytical low-frequency focus
main_result: finite size, diffusion, and Coulomb correlation modify GR current noise
limitations: not bipolar; ideal contacts; not HgCdTe-specific
novelty_implication: finite-size Poisson-plus-diffusion GR noise is established

title: Current and voltage noise spectrum due to generation and recombination fluctuations in semiconductors
verified_location: Eqs. 1-17; current-noise Sec. III; voltage-noise Sec. IV; asymptotes and conclusion
geometry: finite sample with ideal metallic contacts
statistics: small fluctuations; Poisson generation and recombination events
transport_model: electron drift-diffusion with dynamic ionized traps
recombination_model: one generation-recombination trap channel represented by independent Poisson generation and recombination processes
contact_boundary_conditions: fixed-voltage absorbing electron boundaries; open-circuit zero-current boundaries
terminal_boundary_condition: separate fixed-voltage current-noise and zero-field open-circuit voltage-noise ensembles
noise_primitives: independent generation and recombination Poisson processes
covariance_basis: each process PSD 2g0; difference source PSD 4g0; independent event positions
psd_convention: ordinary frequency f with transfer functions at s=2 pi i f; one-sided engineering normalization
main_assumptions: uniform coefficients; unipolar; ideal contacts; linearized fluctuations; nonlinear Ei*n term neglected
main_result: full-frequency finite-length current and voltage spectra; long-sample Lorentzian; finite diffusion/screening spectra; high-frequency voltage PSD proportional to f^-7/2
limitations: no holes; no optical profile; no finite stochastic contacts; no single-particle diffusion autocorrelation
relevance_to_r06: closest general-theory benchmark for finite drift-diffusion-Poisson-trap terminal spectra
novelty_implication: broad coupled finite-device full-frequency premise is occupied

title: Generation-recombination noise in uniformly doped semiconductors with contacts of finite surface recombination velocities
verified_location: publisher abstract only; full paper unavailable
physical_model: majority-carrier GR noise with finite contact velocity
noise_primitives: bulk GR; stochastic contact primitive not verifiable
main_result: field and contact velocity modify finite-sample GR spectrum
limitations: full equations unavailable; contact detailed balance cannot be assessed
novelty_implication: not a blocking dependency after independent full interface-noise audits

title: Generation-recombination noise modelling in semiconductor devices through population or approximate equivalent current density fluctuations
verified_location: Abstract; Introduction; Secs. 2-4; conclusion; coefficient transcription pending
physical_model: fundamental population versus equivalent current sources
recombination_model: direct and SRH with trap occupation
noise_primitives: electron, hole, and trap population sources
covariance_basis: forward-plus-reverse transition activity with electron-hole-trap coupling
psd_convention: frequency-domain voltage PSD; exact normalization pending
main_assumptions: finite uniformly doped sample; bipolar drift-diffusion; direct or SRH transitions
main_result: equivalent-current and monopolar sources fail when boundaries or minority carriers matter
limitations: not HgCdTe-specific; exact coefficient-level transcription incomplete
relevance_to_r06: basis for bipolar primitive-source and no-double-counting rules

title: Extension of the impedance field method to the noise analysis of a semiconductor junction: Analytical approach
authors: O. M. Bulashenko; G. Gomila; J. M. Rubi; V. A. Kochelap
verified_location: Eq. 1; drift-diffusion model; impedance-field derivation; Eq. 35; Figs. 2-8
physical_model: local and terminal noise in an inhomogeneous semiconductor junction
transport_model: drift and diffusion; analytical impedance field
electrostatics: nonuniform field and charge; displacement current present in full current equation but neglected in low-frequency transfer solution
contact_boundary_conditions: junction and sample-contact regions embedded in stationary profile
covariance_basis: local source weighted by impedance field; field and sample-contact cross terms
psd_convention: one-sided thermal-noise convention; exact project conversion required
main_assumptions: known stationary profiles; Markovian local source; frequency below dielectric relaxation
main_result: analytical terminal transfer; cross terms are necessary to recover local Nyquist 4kBT R(x)
limitations: unipolar; no GR traps or optical generation; no stochastic finite-S boundary; quasistatic noise solution
relevance_to_r06: independent adjoint/impedance-field benchmark and FDT cross-term requirement
novelty_implication: transfer-field architecture and local contribution maps are established

title: Fluctuations generated at semiconductor interfaces
verified_location: Eqs. 1-5; Eqs. 19 and 29; Eqs. 30-36; Eqs. 43-55; Eqs. 68-72; conclusion
physical_model: nonlinear stochastic carrier exchange at ideal and nonideal semiconductor interfaces
geometry: zero-thickness interface with optional localized states
statistics: nondegenerate specializations plus general activity formulation
recombination_model: interface states treated as explicit exchange population
contact_boundary_conditions: fluctuating boundary conditions assembled from q1<->q2 q1<->qs q2<->qs processes
terminal_boundary_condition: local interface currents and optional interface-state storage
covariance_basis: mean forward-minus-reverse and covariance forward-plus-reverse; composite cross covariance from shared primitive processes
psd_convention: time-domain delta covariance; one/two-sided spectral conversion not explicit
main_assumptions: quasistationary internal coordinate; independent elementary activated processes
main_result: stochastic interface boundary theory including nonlinear generalized FDT and interface-state cross covariance
limitations: unipolar; no complete finite-device terminal spectrum; no optical generation or bipolar pair recombination
relevance_to_r06: authoritative contact-event covariance and interface-state benchmark
novelty_implication: stochastic semiconductor-interface exchange and shared-event cross covariance are established

title: Local noise analysis of a Schottky contact: Combined thermionic-emission-diffusion theory
authors: G. Gomila; O. M. Bulashenko; J. M. Rubi
verified_location: Eqs. 1-12; Eqs. 18-31; Eqs. 33-40; Appendix A; Figs. 1-8
physical_model: thermionic-emission and diffusion noise in a Schottky diode
geometry: metal-semiconductor contact plus long semiconductor bulk
electrostatics: Poisson depletion layer; displacement in full current but low-frequency noise reduction
contact_boundary_conditions: thermionic-emission boundary J=q(n0-n0eq)vrA; ideal ohmic far contact
terminal_boundary_condition: constant-current terminal voltage noise and derived current noise
noise_primitives: distributed diffusion-current source K=4q^2nD and thermionic surface source SIc=2q(I+2Ic)
covariance_basis: primitive surface and bulk sources independent; depletion-series output cross term from shared distributed bulk source
main_assumptions: long sample L>>LD; frequency below dielectric relaxation; constant mobility examples
main_result: closed impedance/noise decomposition; equilibrium contact and local bulk Nyquist recovery; thermal-shot crossover
limitations: unipolar; no GR traps; no optical generation; low-frequency plateau; no interface states
relevance_to_r06: mandatory stochastic Schottky and transfer-cross-term benchmark
novelty_implication: thermionic contact noise and equilibrium cross-term requirements are established

title: Effects of blocking contacts on generation-recombination noise and responsivity in intrinsic photoconductors
physical_model: intrinsic photoconductor GR noise with variable contact recombination
recombination_model: single bulk lifetime plus deterministic contact loss
contact_boundary_conditions: Robin minority-carrier flux with left/right velocities S' and S; S->0 blocking; S->infinity absorbing
terminal_boundary_condition: constant-current terminal voltage noise with spatial-average and Dember boundary terms
noise_primitives: thermal and background population fluctuations; no explicit contact exchange source
covariance_basis: Smith population covariance propagated by finite-S Green function
psd_convention: one-sided cosine Wiener-Khinchin spectrum in angular frequency with bandwidth in Hz
main_assumptions: ambipolar one-population model; deterministic contacts; constant coefficients
main_result: blocking contacts increase responsivity and noise, lower rolloff frequency, and can move detectivity toward BLIP
limitations: no Poisson; no bipolar state; no trap state; no stochastic contact covariance; 1D effective contacts
novelty_implication: finite deterministic contact velocity and blocking-contact noise effects are established; stochastic contact closure remains candidate gap

title: Theory of generation-recombination noise in intrinsic photoconductors
physical_model: finite intrinsic-photoconductor GR noise with drift, diffusion, absorbing contacts, and spatial covariance
material: generic intrinsic photoconductor; x approximately 0.21 HgCdTe numerical example
recombination_model: one bulk lifetime; thermal and background generation
noise_primitives: thermal equilibrium and background-generated population fluctuations
psd_convention: one-sided cosine Wiener-Khinchin spectrum in angular frequency with bandwidth in Hz
main_assumptions: equal excess electron and hole densities; ideal contacts; constant coefficients
main_result: spatial source-lifetime correlation, drift, and diffusion produce field-dependent non-Lorentzian HgCdTe noise; PSD asymptotes -3/2 thermal and -2 background
limitations: no Poisson; no explicit traps; no finite contact velocity or contact source; constant-current ensemble
novelty_implication: finite drift-diffusion HgCdTe GR noise and non-Lorentzian spatial effects are established

title: Theory of deep level trap effects on generation-recombination noise in HgCdTe photoconductors
authors: A. Evan Iverson; D. L. Smith
geometry: finite photoconductor with ohmic end contacts
transport_model: quasineutral ambipolar drift-diffusion with trap-modified mobility and diffusivity
recombination_model: band-to-band plus four deep-level capture/emission channels
noise_primitives: band-to-band thermal generation; background photon generation; bound-free trap exchange
covariance_basis: explicit zero-time free-carrier and trap population covariance, including electron-trap cross correlation
psd_convention: one-sided cosine spectrum; Green transform exp(-i omega t); bandwidth in Hz
main_assumptions: uniform low-level n-type material; one two-state trap; quasineutrality
main_result: deep traps alter lifetime and sweepout and add a separate trap thermal-noise source
limitations: no self-consistent Poisson; ideal contacts; no stochastic contact source; selected trap parameters
novelty_implication: dynamic trap populations, correlated trap/carrier fluctuations, and trap thermal noise in HgCdTe are established

title: Two-dimensional modelling of HgCdTe photoconductive detectors
authors: E. P. G. Smith; C. A. Musca; L. Faraone
geometry: practical planar contacts versus ideal end contacts
main_result: planar geometry changes fields and carriers; 2D fit did not require fitted S
relevance_to_r06: limits interpretation of a 1D contact velocity
novelty_implication: 1D is an idealized regime map, not a direct planar prediction

title: Investigation of the bulk and surface electronic properties of HgCdTe epitaxial layers using photoelectromagnetic, Hall, and photoconductivity measurements
authors: G. Sarusi; A. Zemel; D. Eger; S. Ron; Y. Shapira
physical_model: joint extraction of transport and recombination parameters
main_result: mobility, diffusion length, lifetime, absorption, and surface velocity versus T; CdTe-capped S below 5000 cm/s at 77 K
limitations: specimen-specific; passivated surface is not a metal contact

title: Noise modeling of shunt resistance in HgCdTe photoconductor detectors
authors: R. K. Bhan; V. Gopal; R. S. Saxena; J. P. Singh
verified_location: abstract; introduction; model overview; experiment; conclusions
physical_model: bulk GR plus passivant-induced shunt noise
noise_primitives: bulk GR and lumped shunt contribution
main_result: bulk GR alone did not fit; surface shunts added noise
relevance_to_r06: alternative explanation for excess or multimode spectra
novelty_implication: departures from lumped GR are not unique evidence of spatial modes

title: Thermal Agitation of Electric Charge in Conductors
verified_location: APS record; convention translation by project
main_result: thermal terminal spectrum linked to dissipative impedance
novelty_implication: FDT recovery is validation, not novelty

title: Thermal Agitation of Electricity in Conductors
Statistics of the Recombinations of Holes and Electrons
authors: W. Shockley; W. T. Read Jr.
verified_location: Fig. 1; Eqs. 2.5-2.9; Eqs. 3.8-3.9; Eqs. 4.1-4.5; lifetime analysis
physical_model: localized capture and emission through a two-charge-state trap
statistics: Fermi-Dirac formulation with nondegenerate band reduction
recombination_model: four channels: electron capture/emission and hole capture/emission
noise_primitives: paper provides mean transition rates; R06 derives primitive event channels from them
covariance_basis: project stoichiometric covariance from four positive propensities; net SRH is not a covariance
main_assumptions: one trap family; fast internal trap relaxation; steady occupancy for algebraic SRH reduction
main_result: steady trap occupancy and net SRH recombination law derived from four forward/reverse transitions
limitations: no transport, electrostatics, contacts, terminal noise, or stochastic covariance
relevance_to_r06: authoritative kinetic basis for dynamic trap propensities
novelty_implication: algebraic SRH and four-channel kinetics are established; stochastic device closure is separate
